response.justification: Strong engineer with proven ability to work on complex systems and deep commitment to software craftsmanship, but fundamentally misaligned with role requirements. Zero documented Spring ecosystem or enterprise framework experience (explicitly required), no Kotlin expertise, and only decade-old Java work at NI LabWindows. Current career trajectory (TypeScript/full-stack/AI agents) shows drift away from JVM specialization. Would require significant ramp-up on core enterprise technologies before meaningful contribution.
strengths: Proven experience with complex systems: built NI LabWindows IDE, high-performance services at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second, and distributed platform (GISCollective)
Deep commitment to software craftsmanship: authored testing frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), consistently applies TDD, emphasizes code quality and maintainability across roles
Excellent communication and mentoring: organized workshops, taught courses, presented at FOSS4G, demonstrates ability to share knowledge and collaborate
concerns: Zero documented experience with Spring ecosystem or enterprise Java frameworks (Quarkus, Hibernate, etc.)—explicitly required for role and not mentioned anywhere in resume
Java work is 10+ years old (NI, 2014-2015); current focus since 2018 is TypeScript/Node.js/full-stack, showing career drift away from JVM specialization and enterprise tooling
No Kotlin experience mentioned; current skills emphasize web/backend languages (JavaScript, D, C#) rather than JVM ecosystem expertise required for IDE development
